title: Quiz: Deployment and Production Considerations
description: Test your understanding of deploying quantum credit scoring models to production

questions.question: Why is a hybrid quantum-classical architecture important for production credit scoring?

options.text: It provides fallback when quantum resources are unavailable
It reduces the need for testing

questions.explanation: Hybrid architecture ensures business continuity by providing classical fallback when quantum hardware is unavailable, in maintenance, or experiencing high queue depths. This is critical for production systems that require high availability.

questions.question: What is the Population Stability Index (PSI) used to measure in model monitoring?

options.text: Shift in the prediction distribution compared to baseline

questions.explanation: PSI measures how much the distribution of model predictions has shifted compared to the baseline distribution. A PSI above 0.2 typically indicates significant drift that requires investigation and potential model retraining.

questions.question: What regulation requires banks to maintain comprehensive model documentation and independent validation?
questions.explanation: SR 11-7 (Supervisory Guidance on Model Risk Management) requires financial institutions to maintain comprehensive model documentation, perform independent validation, and establish ongoing monitoring for all models used in decision-making.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a canary deployment for credit models?

options.text: To test the model with synthetic data
To gradually route traffic to new model while monitoring performance

questions.explanation: Canary deployment routes a small percentage of traffic (typically 1-10%) to the new model while monitoring its performance. This allows early detection of issues before full rollout, minimizing risk to the business.

questions.question: FCRA requires credit models to provide adverse action reasons when a credit application is denied.
questions.explanation: True. The Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA) requires lenders to provide specific reasons when taking adverse action (like denying credit). This means credit scoring models must be able to generate explanations for their decisions.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a circuit breaker pattern in quantum credit scoring services?

options.text: To prevent electrical damage to quantum hardware
To automatically disable quantum backend when error rate exceeds threshold

questions.explanation: A circuit breaker pattern monitors error rates and automatically falls back to classical processing when the quantum backend experiences too many failures. This prevents cascade failures and maintains service availability.

questions.question: Why is feature hashing used in audit logs for credit decisions?

options.text: To protect customer privacy while maintaining auditability

questions.explanation: Feature hashing allows audit logs to maintain a verifiable record of what data was used for each decision without storing sensitive customer information in plain text. This balances regulatory auditability requirements with privacy protection.

questions.question: Which are key components of a production quantum credit scoring monitoring system? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Production monitoring must include prediction drift detection (PSI), feature distribution monitoring (to catch data quality issues), and fairness metrics tracking (for regulatory compliance). Marketing analysis is unrelated to model monitoring.
